anomalies.explanation: Collect a valid 12-digit TIN from the counter-party before claiming any related input VAT on Mushak 9.1.
anomalies.legal_reference: Value Added Tax and Supplementary Duty Act, 2012 - input tax credit admissible only against a registered person with a valid TIN
anomalies.default_suggestion: Collect a valid 12-digit TIN from the counter-party before claiming any related input VAT on Mushak 9.1.

anomalies.explanation: Re-issue the invoice with VAT recomputed at the standard 15% rate, update Mushak 6.6, and re-submit Mushak 9.1 for the period. Effective rate observed: 10.00% (base 200000.00, VAT 20000.00).
anomalies.legal_reference: Value Added Tax and Supplementary Duty Act, 2012 - standard VAT rate is 15%
anomalies.default_suggestion: Re-issue the invoice with VAT recomputed at the standard 15% rate, update Mushak 6.6, and re-submit Mushak 9.1 for the period.

anomalies.explanation: Re-issue the invoice with VAT recomputed at the standard 15% rate, update Mushak 6.6, and re-submit Mushak 9.1 for the period. Effective rate observed: 10.00% (base 200000.00, VAT 20000.00).

anomalies.explanation: Reverse the negative invoice total and record the sales return as a Mushak 6.2 credit note in Mushak 6.6 and Mushak 9.1.
anomalies.legal_reference: VAT & SD Rules, 2016 - sales returns require a separate credit note, not a negative invoice
anomalies.default_suggestion: Reverse the negative invoice total and record the sales return as a Mushak 6.2 credit note in Mushak 6.6 and Mushak 9.1.

anomalies.explanation: Reconcile the invoice total so it equals value_excl_vat + VAT, then re-submit Mushak 9.1.
anomalies.legal_reference: Value Added Tax and Supplementary Duty Act, 2012 - declared total must reconcile with line components
anomalies.default_suggestion: Reconcile the invoice total so it equals value_excl_vat + VAT, then re-submit Mushak 9.1.

anomalies.explanation: Collect the Mushak 6.3 Tax Challan reference for the purchase before claiming related input VAT on Mushak 9.1.
anomalies.legal_reference: Value Added Tax and Supplementary Duty Act, 2012, section 46 - input VAT rebate is inadmissible without the corresponding Mushak 6.3 Tax Challan reference
anomalies.default_suggestion: Collect the Mushak 6.3 Tax Challan reference for the purchase before claiming related input VAT on Mushak 9.1.

anomalies.explanation: Correct the TIN to a 12-digit numeric value; verify against NBR's TIN database.
anomalies.legal_reference: NBR TIN registration rules - TIN must be exactly 12 numeric digits
anomalies.default_suggestion: Correct the TIN to a 12-digit numeric value; verify against NBR's TIN database.

anomalies.explanation: Renumber the duplicate invoice so each line in the register is unique, then re-submit Mushak 9.1. Invoice 'INV-S-1003' appears 2 times in the sales register.
anomalies.legal_reference: VAT & SD Rules, 2016 - each Mushak 9.1 line must have a unique invoice reference
anomalies.default_suggestion: Renumber the duplicate invoice so each line in the register is unique, then re-submit Mushak 9.1.

anomalies.explanation: Renumber the duplicate invoice so each line in the register is unique, then re-submit Mushak 9.1. Invoice 'INV-S-1003' appears 2 times in the sales register.

anomalies.expected: distinct invoice numbering between sales and purchase
anomalies.explanation: Investigate the invoice appearing in both registers; if it is recycled, remove it from the offending register and file the correct transaction in Mushak 9.1. Invoice 'INV-S-1003' appears in both sales and purchase registers (sales ∩ purchase = {INV-S-1003}).
anomalies.legal_reference: Value Added Tax and Supplementary Duty Act, 2012, section 46 - circular or recycled invoices may lead to disallowance of input tax credit
anomalies.default_suggestion: Investigate the invoice appearing in both registers; if it is recycled, remove it from the offending register and file the correct transaction in Mushak 9.1.
